title: Port remaining grammar scanners: Python, TypeScript, Rust, C, C++
description:
Generate grammar packages and port external scanners for the remaining high-priority languages.

This task covers multiple grammars that follow the same pattern established by Go, JavaScript, and Bash scanner ports. Each grammar needs: tsgo-generate output, external scanner port (if any), and differential testing.

Languages and scanner complexity:
- Python: indentation-sensitive scanner (indent/dedent/newline tracking)
- TypeScript: extends JavaScript scanner, ~1000 lines
- Rust: relatively simple scanner (raw strings, block comments)
- C: minimal scanner (if any)
- C++: complex scanner (~2000 lines) — may use CGo fallback if too complex

Deliverables per grammar:
- Generated grammar package via tsgo-generate
- External scanner ported to Go (or CGo fallback for C++)
- Differential test passing against C tree-sitter on representative corpus

Tests:
- Parse real-world files from popular repos for each language
- Differential test against C tree-sitter
- Stress test TypeScript with large grammar tables (~4000 lex states)

Reference: docs/design.md §4 External Scanners
